title: teucrium salviastrum subsp. rubriflorum
intro: Teucrium salviastrum subsp. rubriflorum, also known as red-flowered germander, is a subspecies of the Teucrium salviastrum plant, which is a type of herbaceous perennial in the family Lamiaceae. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Teucrium salviastrum subsp. rubriflorum include reducing stress, improving cognitive function, and aiding in the treatment of digestive issues, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Teucrium salviastrum subsp. rubriflorum can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is also used in traditional medicine to make salves and ointments for topical use.
